completeness_goals.goal: Solution must wire dynamic registration end-to-end, not stage surface gestures. Concretely: (a) the three registration helpers must mutate the module-level structures in their home modules (not local copies, not test-only monkey-patches, not import-time aliases that lose freshness); (b) generic_lang() must invoke those helpers as part of its normal call flow (a stub generic_lang that does nothing, or one that hard-codes if/elif over known language ids, fails); (c) helpers must not raise on the call paths exercised by F2P or by self-scan. Token-match shims (definitions exist but bodies are 'pass' or 'raise NotImplementedError'), fixers wired by name but with no callable, or langs.py changes that satisfy a grep but don't filter shared phase labels at runtime all fail this floor. The following known-bad effort patterns MUST also fail this goal regardless of any other surface signal: (i) GIT-OPS EXFILTRATION — diffs whose only substantive change is a checkout/reset/cherry-pick/apply of an external sha, branch, or downloaded patchfile (i.e. the agent fetched the gold instead of authoring it); (ii) ZERO-EDIT CAPTURES — final.patch is empty, whitespace-only, or contains only file-permission/mode bits with no line-level content additions in the named target modules (registry.py, scoring_internal/policy/core.py, narrative/_constants.py, languages/framework/generic.py, app/commands/langs.py); (iii) COMMENT-ONLY DIFFS — the diff contributes only comments, docstrings, blank lines, or import re-ordering in the target modules with no executable statement added to the named helpers or to generic_lang().
completeness_goals.rationale: Provenance: Step 1 + Step 2 in the user's plan. The instruction frames Step 2 as 'Wire generic_lang() to register detectors'; helpers without wiring (or wiring without real module-level mutation) defeats every downstream consumer the user named (scoring, narrative, langs command). The three known-bad patterns (git-ops exfiltration, zero-edit captures, comment-only diffs) are the canonical anti-effort floor required across the v3 rubric suite — naming them explicitly here prevents a hollow-diff trial from co-passing on coincidental keyword matches in test.sh.
completeness_goals.anti_gaming: If any goal_1..goal_6 evidence is 'function exists' / 'import succeeds' without observable mutation visible to a reader holding a prior reference, this goal fails — preventing co-pass on hollow shims. Additionally, presence of any of the three known-bad effort patterns (git-ops exfiltration, zero-edit captures, comment-only diffs) is an automatic failure of this goal even if downstream goals appear satisfied — this defeats trials that fetched the gold and the judge would otherwise score the resulting tree as correct.

completeness_goals.goal: Three runtime-registration primitives exist in the canonical modules named by the user (registry, scoring policy, narrative constants) and behave correctly: (a) the detector-registration helper inserts a DetectorMeta into the module-level DETECTORS dict and appends to the display-order list idempotently (re-registering the same name does not duplicate the display-order entry); (b) the scoring-policy-registration helper inserts a DetectorScoringPolicy into DETECTOR_SCORING_POLICIES and rebuilds derived state so that DIMENSIONS_BY_NAME reflects the new detector AND FILE_BASED_DETECTORS contains file_based=True entries while excluding file_based=False entries; (c) the narrative DETECTOR_TOOLS refresh helper resyncs DETECTOR_TOOLS from current DETECTORS via in-place mutation, so a caller holding a reference to the prior dict object observes the new entries.
completeness_goals.rationale: Provenance: instruction.md Step 1 (three named subsections — registry.py, scoring_internal/policy/core.py, narrative/_constants.py). Idempotence is required by the Step 1 prose; in-place mutation is explicitly called out ('Mutating in-place ensures all references see updates'). The dimension/file_based rebuild semantics are explicit in the Step 1 code block for _rebuild_derived.
completeness_goals.anti_gaming: Idempotence (no _DISPLAY_ORDER duplication on re-register), the file_based=False exclusion check, and the prior-reference in-place observability check together rule out: (i) appending without dedup; (ii) marking every detector file_based; (iii) rebinding DETECTOR_TOOLS to a new dict (breaks readers that captured the old reference).

completeness_goals.goal: A single call to the generic-language factory with one or more tool specs causes each tool's id to be observable in all three downstream structures without any further manual call: the detector registry, the scoring-policy map (and its derived view DIMENSIONS_BY_NAME under the 'Code quality' dimension AND FILE_BASED_DETECTORS), and the narrative DETECTOR_TOOLS map. The three writes must be a consequence of invoking generic_lang(name=..., tools=[...]), not require post-hoc bookkeeping at call sites.
completeness_goals.rationale: Provenance: instruction.md Step 2 (the user-supplied code block shows register_detector + register_scoring_policy + refresh_detector_tools all called from inside generic_lang for each tool). These three are observable consequences of one wiring decision and therefore one goal, not three independently-earnable slices.
completeness_goals.anti_gaming: The three structures must co-populate from one call. Wiring that registers detectors but never calls the policy registrar (so DIMENSIONS_BY_NAME / FILE_BASED_DETECTORS don't update) fails; wiring that registers everything but skips refresh_detector_tools (so narrative is stale) fails; wiring that requires the caller to invoke a helper after generic_lang fails. Dimension MUST be 'Code quality' per the user's explicit choice in Step 2.

completeness_goals.goal: The language config produced by the generic-language factory includes shared cross-language analysis phases on top of the tool-specific phases. Concretely, the resulting LangConfig.phases contains a security-related phase plus at least one of: subjective review, duplicate detection, or boilerplate (duplication) detection, with a total phase count of at least three (tool phase + security + ≥1 shared). The shared phases must degrade gracefully when language-specific machinery (function extraction, dep graph) is unavailable — scanning a generic plugin must not crash because extract_functions returns [].
completeness_goals.rationale: Provenance: instruction.md Step 3 explicitly lists 'security, boilerplate duplication, subjective review, duplicates' as shared phases to append. The 'Graceful degradation' bullet list in Step 3 prose explicitly requires noop extract_functions, empty dep_graph, and missing-tool fallthrough to not crash. test_coverage is explicitly excluded by the user ('What's NOT included').
completeness_goals.anti_gaming: Requires ≥3 total phases AND security AND ≥1 of three named shared phases — defeats a shim that adds one decorative phase. Graceful-degradation clause rejects a 'security phase that crashes on empty file_finder' (still passes the count check but breaks self-scan / F2P import path).

completeness_goals.goal: Tool specs accept an optional fix_cmd key. When a tool spec carries fix_cmd, the generic-language factory constructs a FixerConfig and adds it under the LangConfig.fixers map keyed by a dashed transformation of the tool id, AND the DetectorMeta registered for that tool sets action_type to the auto-fix variant rather than manual-fix. Tool specs without fix_cmd produce no fixer entry and keep the manual-fix action_type. At least one shipped language plugin (e.g. ruby, go, rust, swift, kotlin) declares fix_cmd in its tool spec so the plumbing is exercised end-to-end.
completeness_goals.rationale: Provenance: instruction.md Step 4 ('Optional Tool Fixers') including the Tool-spec-extension code block, the _make_generic_fixer factory, the Wire-into-generic_lang block with action_type='auto_fix', and the plugin table listing go/rust/ruby/swift/kotlin as fix_cmd carriers (with elixir/php/cxx/bash/perl/lua explicitly 'none'). T4 ('what about all the other tools?') confirms the user expects plumbing to apply across plugins, not just one.
completeness_goals.anti_gaming: Both halves required (fixer entry AND action_type flip); a fixer dict populated under the wrong key or with action_type unchanged fails. Requires ≥1 shipped plugin file to actually declare fix_cmd — defeats a plumbing-only change with no live plugin exercising it.

completeness_goals.goal: The langs command's tool-listing logic distinguishes tool-specific phases from shared framework phases when assembling its displayed tool list. Specifically: when iterating phases to produce the tool labels for a generic plugin, the four shared-phase labels (security, subjective review, boilerplate duplication, duplicates) are excluded from the displayed list. Additionally, when a LangConfig has at least one fixer, the displayed tool listing is annotated with an auto-fix indicator (e.g., an appended ' (auto-fix)' suffix or equivalent disambiguator visible to a reader of the langs output).
completeness_goals.rationale: Provenance: instruction.md Step 5 ('Update langs Command') with the user-provided _get_tool_labels code block enumerating the four shared labels and the (auto-fix) suffix.
completeness_goals.anti_gaming: Both halves required (filtering AND auto-fix suffix). Filtering must apply at runtime to the langs output, not just exist as a constant in a module — a constant SHARED_PHASE_LABELS that no code reads fails this goal. The four shared labels are exactly the four the user named in Step 3, ruling out a partial filter list.

completeness_goals.goal: There is some observable per-language quality or capability indicator that (a) can be computed for any registered language plugin and reports something more granular than a binary 'works/doesn't' — for instance a score, grade, depth label, or capability summary keyed off the LangConfig — and (b) is surfaced as part of the scan command's output (printed to stdout, written to scan state/JSON, or otherwise visible to a user invoking the scan subcommand on a generic-plugin language). Implementation form is open: a score-and-grade pair, a coverage percentage, a capability checklist, or any equivalent representation satisfies the goal as long as it is computed from the LangConfig and emitted by the scan command.
completeness_goals.rationale: Provenance: USER turn T5 ('do we track the quality of each language implementation?') + T6 ('yes, is that included in the scan?'). T5 establishes the feature; T6 specifies the integration point (scan output). The user did not name an implementation, so the goal is framed behaviorally — any indicator that satisfies the two halves passes. Absent from v2 (Phase A coverage fix). Low weight because the user's affirmation in T6 is brief and the session was cut off before further specification.
completeness_goals.anti_gaming: Both halves required (computable AND surfaced in scan output). A capability dataclass that exists but is never printed/written by the scan command fails the (b) half. A scan-command print that just echoes the plugin name with no LangConfig-derived signal fails the (a) half. Implementation-agnostic — does NOT require any specific symbol name (avoids reward for matching oracle's assess_lang_quality).

completeness_goals.goal: Pre-existing imports and scan paths remain functional after the changes. Importing DETECTORS, DetectorMeta, DETECTOR_SCORING_POLICIES, DetectorScoringPolicy, and DETECTOR_TOOLS from their canonical modules must not raise. The python plugin (the self-scan target) must continue to scan without crashing, and its registry/scoring/narrative behavior must not regress for detectors that existed before the change.
completeness_goals.rationale: Provenance: instruction.md Verification block ('Self-scan still works: python -m desloppify --lang python scan --path desloppify') and the implicit floor in 'We already have 22 generic language plugins' — the user expects existing functionality preserved. test.sh has an explicit P2P regression import gate.
completeness_goals.anti_gaming: Modeled separately from F2Ps so that a passing F2P suite cannot still score 1.0 if base imports break (e.g., a registration helper that imports a module-not-yet-registered at module load time crashes registry imports for everyone).

decomposition_notes: v3 rewrites v2 to satisfy four constraints simultaneously: (1) every goal traces to a USER turn (instruction.md or a numbered follow-up); (2) goal weights differ from F2P weights to reflect conceptual importance not test-side allocation; (3) no oracle-only identifier is used as a hard rubric requirement — goal_quality_tracking_in_scan is framed behaviorally so any score/grade/depth/checklist representation satisfies it; (4) anti-gaming clauses inside each goal name concrete shim patterns that fail, supplementing the goal_0 floor. Eight goals total (v2 had seven). Coverage additions: Step 4 fixer plumbing, Step 5 langs command filtering, T5+T6 quality-in-scan. Collapse retained from v2: F2P-4/5/6 → goal_generic_lang_wiring (three observable consequences of one wiring decision). The session's later follow-up turns (T7 aesthetics, T8/T9/T10 tree-sitter planning) remain unscored — T7 is a subjective probe with no concrete deliverable, T8-T10 trail off into planning that was cut off mid-message with zero tree-sitter implementation code reaching the diff. The reference_patch.json remains _status: no_canonical (Tier 2 stub) — this rubric is the sole structural ground truth.
